identity.name: Ink & Ember
identity.mission: An offline-first worldbuilding and narrative management platform for archivists, DMs, and writers — with optional online collaboration and Foundry VTT integration.

positioning.maintenance_philosophy: Self-contained. No required server, no subscriptions, no forced logins.

anti_patterns: Cloud-first dark patterns (forced accounts, forced sync, forced telemetry)

rules: Offline-first is non-negotiable: everything works without internet.
Online collaboration is an opt-in enhancement, never a requirement.
No feature may require an account unless it is explicitly a collaboration feature.
The app should feel like handling an artifact: tactile, deliberate, readable.
Prioritize clarity over cleverness. Prefer fewer UI elements with stronger meaning.
Fast local search is a core feature, not a nice-to-have.

microcopy: Errors should be calm and actionable, not snarky.

rules: Use 'Cormorant Garamond' for article titles in Fantasy/Archivist themes.
Use 'Space Grotesk' for headers in Void theme.
Do NOT use Inter for headings.
Body text must be at least 16px (text-base).
Long-form content (Manuscripts) must use 'Merriweather' with leading-relaxed.
Use 'Caveat' for user notes/annotations to simulate marginalia.
Use JetBrains Mono for IDs, hashes, code blocks, Foundry UUIDs, and export logs.

ink_and_ember.description: Default brand theme. Deep obsidian blacks with ember-orange accents.

the_archivist.description: Paper-like warmth. Iron-gall browns, vellum whites, bookbinding accents.

void_state.description: High-contrast sci-fi/terminal. Sharp corners, neon accents, precision UI.

faewild.description: Organic, mysterious, deep forest tones with gilded highlights.

textures: Add a subtle noise overlay (opacity 0.03) to all backgrounds to prevent digital flatness.
Use 'ink splash' masks for images in Ink & Ember theme.
Use 'paper grain' patterns for The Archivist theme.
Void theme: allow scanline/noise at very low opacity (<= 0.02) but never gradients.
borders: Ink & Ember: 1px solid #2a2a2a with subtle ember glow on active elements.
Archivist: Double borders for 'important' documents to mimic leather binding.
Faewild: 1px solid #2d4a3e with faint gold highlight on hover.
shadows: Archivist: soft warm shadows (shadow-md) with low opacity.
Void: minimal shadows; rely on borders and contrast.
Faewild: soft shadow plus slight inner glow for magical emphasis (subtle).

rules: Animations must be subtle and purposeful (no bouncy UI).
Respect prefers-reduced-motion (disable non-essential motion).
Use 150–220ms for micro-interactions, 250–350ms for page transitions.
patterns: Image zoom on hover for article covers (scale 1.02–1.05).
Popover fade + slight lift for tooltips and timeline details.

wiki_container.structure: Sidebar (250px fixed) + Main Content (fluid) + Infobox (300px hidden on mobile)
rules: Main content max-width: 80ch for optimal reading.
Infobox floats right on desktop, stacks at bottom on mobile.
Breadcrumbs are mandatory for deep navigation.
Every page must have a 'Last Edited' + 'Location' meta line (even offline).
Prefer fewer, larger cards. Avoid dense widgets.
Each card gets a single primary action.

timeline_view.style: Horizontal scroll container with sticky 'Era' headers.
timeline_view.interaction: Pinch-to-zoom or slider to change granularity (Century -> Year -> Day).
rules: Events must be readable at a glance: date, title, type badge.
Hover reveals summary; click opens full entry panel.

library_view.style: Index-card catalog list with filters and fast keyboard navigation.
rules: Search bar always visible on desktop.
Cmd/Ctrl+K opens global search palette.
Results show type, tags, last edited, and parent path.

ArticleCard.description: Card with image cover, title, type badge, and 'last edited' meta.
ArticleCard.style: Overflow hidden, image zoom on hover, strong typography.

TimelineNode.description: Point on the timeline. Hover reveals summary popover.
TimelineNode.style: Circle marker with connecting line. Color coded by 'Event Type'.

WikiSidebar.description: Collapsible tree navigation for article categories.
WikiSidebar.style: Sticky position. Accordion style for categories.

ThemeSwitcher.description: Dropdown to select one of the themes.

GlobalSearch.description: Cmd/Ctrl+K command palette for instant navigation and creation.

FoundryExportPanel.description: Export and package selected entities for Foundry VTT integration.
FoundryExportPanel.style: Step-by-step but minimal; shows warnings, UUID mapping, and output logs.

storage.default: Local project vault stored on device.
rules: All reads/writes must succeed offline.
Autosave is required, with local version snapshots.
Provide a clear manual 'Export Vault' action.
Collaboration features must be clearly labeled as 'Online'.
Never block editing if sync fails; queue changes locally.
Conflicts must be readable and resolvable (diff view).
No telemetry by default.
If analytics exist, they must be opt-in and local-first (prefer local logs).
Never upload user content without explicit action.

goals: Export lore entities as Foundry-ready compendium packs or JSON payloads.
Preserve stable IDs/UUID mappings for re-import and updates.
Support image assets packaging with references maintained.
ui_rules: Foundry tools live under a dedicated 'Integrations' area.
Show clear compatibility notes per Foundry version/system target.
Provide a dry-run validation step before export.
terminology: Expose UUIDs in a monospace style.
Warn about breaking changes when renaming IDs.

general: Avoid 'AI Slop': no generic gradients, no Inter for headings.
Dark colors must be solid + subtle noise, not gradients.
Use 2–3x more spacing than typical SaaS apps.
All interactive elements must have data-testid.
Prefer keyboard-first UX for power features (search, create, navigate).
accessibility: Meet APCA contrast targets (and WCAG as a baseline).
All interactive elements must have visible focus states.
Ensure readable line length (max 80ch) and comfortable line-height.
engineering: Theme tokens must be CSS variables (no hard-coded colors in components).
Components must be theme-agnostic by default.
Never block on network for core flows.
Provide deterministic exports (same inputs => same output).

instructions_to_main_agent: Implement themes using CSS variables in index.css plus a ThemeContext. Default to 'ink_and_ember'. Build the Wiki layout as the core navigation structure with breadcrumbs. Treat offline-first storage as the default path; online collaboration must be opt-in and non-blocking. Add a Foundry export panel under Integrations with dry-run validation, UUID display, and deterministic outputs.
